value.name: Thoth
value.description: The ibis-headed god of wisdom and magic who restored the damaged eye, making it 'whole' (wadjet). Thoth is said to have supplied the missing 1/64th through magic.
value._unverifiedReason: Target entity not found in database

value.name: Set
value.description: The god of chaos and storms who tore out or damaged the eye during his battle with Horus for the throne of Egypt.
value._unverifiedReason: Target entity not found in database

value.name: Ra
value.description: The sun god whose Eye (Eye of Ra) is sometimes conflated with or contrasted to the Eye of Horus, representing solar versus lunar aspects.
value._unverifiedReason: Target entity not found in database

value.name: Osiris
value.description: Father of Horus, to whom the restored Eye was offered to bring him back to life in the underworld.
value._unverifiedReason: Target entity not found in database

value.name: Eye of Ra
value.description: The solar eye, representing Ra's fierce, destructive aspect. While the Eye of Horus is healing and protective, the Eye of Ra is wrathful and punishing.
value._unverifiedReason: Target entity not found in database

value.name: Uraeus
value.description: The rearing cobra symbol of royal protection, closely connected to the Wadjet eye in royal iconography.
value._unverifiedReason: Target entity not found in database
